L1.area: Native FFI bindings and database extension API wrappers

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a temporary string object is converted to a raw C pointer and immediately deallocated, leaving a dangling pointer that is subsequently passed to a native API function call. This can lead to memory corruption, potentially allowing an attacker to achieve arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Native FFI bindings and database extension API wrappers
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a temporary string object is converted to a raw C pointer and immediately deallocated, leaving a dangling pointer that is subsequently passed to a native API function call. This can lead to memory corruption, potentially allowing an attacker to achieve arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: In the session extension bindings, temporary CString objects created by str_to_cstring() are immediately converted to raw pointers via .as_ptr() in the same expression, causing the CString to be dropped while the pointer is still used in subsequent FFI calls.
